mechanism_narrative: Sustained economic growth, as measured by GDP, indicates a healthy corporate earnings environment, which typically supports positive equity market performance over a longer horizon. Investors anticipate future earnings growth and bid up stock prices.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth correlates with corporate profits
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth is consistently positive (e.g., > 2% annualized) but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative more than 50% of the time over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds a certain threshold, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy stance that increases the cost of capital and reduces corporate profitability expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as discount rates rise.
evidence_refs: fact:Monetary policy tightening impacts equity valuations
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS consistently above 1.00% leads to positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 40% of occurrences over a 3-year rolling window, or if the average return is not statistically negative.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong job creation (high PAYEMS growth) and a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a robust labor market. This supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns as investors anticipate strong earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong labor market supports consumer demand
failure_condition: If periods of strong PAYEMS growth and low UNRATE are consistently followed by negative S&P 500 20-day returns, or if the combined signal's predictive power is not significantly better than individual proxies.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recovery, an acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strengthening consumer confidence and spending. This directly translates to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: If RSAFS acceleration during a recovery regime does not lead to statistically significant positive S&P 500 5-day returns (t-test p > 0.05) in at least 70% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contraction in manufacturing and overall economic activity. This leads to reduced corporate profits and negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors price in an economic slowdown.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines by more than 0.5% over 3 months but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 30% of such instances over any 5-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins. This leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors demand higher risk premiums and central banks tighten policy.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL consistently above 2% leads to positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations over a 5-year rolling window, or if the average return is not statistically negative.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of declining housing starts (HOUST) and a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates a significant slowdown in interest-rate sensitive sectors and tighter financial conditions. This negatively impacts overall economic growth and S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average change < -5% AND FEDFUNDS 3-month average change > 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is sensitive to interest rates
failure_condition: If HOUST declines and FEDFUNDS rises as specified, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 35% of such periods over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a weakening labor market and potential recession. This leads to reduced consumer confidence and corporate earnings, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns as economic outlook deteriorates.
failure_condition: If UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such instances over any 3-year rolling window.
